title: Sweet Corn Soup

ingredients.name: sweet corn kernels (fresh, canned or frozen)
visual_checks: about 2 large cobs worth; canned and drained works perfectly
ingredients.notes: about 2 large cobs worth; canned and drained works perfectly

subs.name: 400 g frozen peas blended partially for a green soup variation
2 tbsp arrowroot powder whisked in cold water — same thickening effect

ingredients.name: spring onion, whites and greens separated

subs.name: 0.5 tsp white pepper for a more authentic Chinese-restaurant flavour
800 ml water with a pinch of turmeric and a bay leaf for colour

steps.text: Set aside 100 g of the corn kernels for texture. Place the remaining 300 g in a blender with 100 ml of the stock and blend to a coarse puree — not too smooth; some texture is good. Strain if you prefer a silky finish, or leave it as is for a more rustic soup.
Whisk cornflour with 4 tbsp cold water until completely lump-free. This is your thickening slurry — add it only at the end.
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add ginger, garlic, spring onion whites and green chilli and sauté for 1 minute until softened and fragrant.
Pour in the remaining stock and bring to a boil. Add the corn puree and the reserved whole kernels and return to a boil. Season with salt, sugar and black pepper.

Stir in the cornflour slurry while the soup is at a steady simmer and cook for 3 minutes, stirring gently, until the soup thickens to a light, pourable cream. It should coat a spoon but flow easily when poured.

Add vinegar if using, taste and adjust salt and sugar. Ladle into bowls and finish with spring onion greens and an extra grind of black pepper. Serve hot with a small bowl of vinegar and chilli sauce on the side for guests to adjust their own heat.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: corn → 400 g frozen peas blended partially for a green soup variation; stock → 800 ml water with a pinch of turmeric and a bay leaf for colour; black-pepper → 0.5 tsp white pepper for a more authentic Chinese-restaurant flavour; cornflour → 2 tbsp arrowroot powder whisked in cold water — same thickening effect
